title: Trivia at the University of Delaware
body:
As a University of Delaware professor this is embarrassing. Not surprising, but embarrassing.

As a research methods professor though i will point out this is a biased subject population. Not biased in the sense of subjects beliefs, biased in that more than likely the students who could easily answer these questions likely aren't going to walk by and participate in this.

title: The Truth About Demeyere Cookware: My Brutally Honest Review
body:
****Products Featured in This Video****

Demeyere Atlantis: https://amzn.to/3k7myl8 (Amazon)
Demeyere Atlantis: https://www.kqzyfj.com/click-8744728-13819538?sid=yt&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.zwilling.com%2Fus%2Fdemeyere%2Fcookware%2Fatlantis%2F (Zwilling.com)
Demeyere Industry: https://amzn.to/3X1s6vX (Amazon)
Demeyere Industry: https://www.kqzyfj.com/click-8744728-13819538?sid=yt&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.zwilling.com%2Fus%2Fdemeyere%2Fcookware%2Findustry-5%2F (Zwilling.com)
All Demeyere Cookware: https://amzn.to/3IFGLZu (Amazon)
All Demeyere Cookware: https://www.kqzyfj.com/click-8744728-13819538?sid=yt&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.zwilling.com%2Fus%2Fdemeyere%2Fcookware%2F (Zwilling.com)
Made In: https://madeincookware.pxf.io/WDx4RJ (MadeInCookware.com)
All-Clad: https://amzn.to/3itLeUu (Amazon)

We may earn a fee if you buy via the affiliate links above (at no extra cost to you). As an Amazon Associate I earn from qualifying purchases.

Read the full review for more details: https://prudentreviews.com/demeyere-review/

Want to know when Demeyere goes on sale? Join our free newsletter to get deal alerts: https://prudentreviews.com/newsletter/
author: @PrudentReviews

title: The Truth About Demeyere Cookware: My Brutally Honest Review
body: I second this reservation wholeheartedly. I own 2 Demeyere pans, Proline 28cm and Atlantis 24cm. Proline arrived with a faulty handle weld on one prong. I had to return it. The replacement was ok. The Atlantis came in with the same issue. One of the weld points (there are 4 altogether) was broken. That's 25% reduction in the structural strength at the place where it is most needed. To make things worse the handle is slightly wonky, the left prong is noticeably closer to the bottom rim than the right one. The performance of both pans is exceptional and I value them highly in that regard. But the whole pleasure of using the pan is seriously diminished by the fault in the handle. There is a serious problem with quality control at Demeyere and people should be aware of it before entering into transactions with them.
author: @magus497

title: The Truth About Demeyere Cookware: My Brutally Honest Review
body: After seeing the video of the guy jumping on it, i got curious and stood on my demeyere pan and it was fine (im 200 lbs) - no issue. Honestly, after having my demeyere pan im kicking myself for having bought an all clad set before having known, and then after learning not buying a set 3 or 4 years ago when i swear they were well under 1,000 for a 10 piece set of industry line pans. These are honestly so much more enjoyable to cook on than tri ply from allclad and the lack of rivets on the inside is honestly SO nice, even if there are some issues with QC. They're owned by henkels now and will honor the warranty, so it's worth rolling the dice considering how pleasurable they are to cook and clean with IMO
author: @youraveragebreakfastcereal5285

title: The Truth About Demeyere Cookware: My Brutally Honest Review
body: I have had my Demeyere pans for 3 years and I love them. The 7 ply construction is heavy, but the benefits are amazing. I have always used carbon steel pans for some cooking that needs a quicker and fine touch. But these pans are the Rolls Royce of stainless steel. And cleanup is almost as easy as nonstick.

title: The Truth About Demeyere Cookware: My Brutally Honest Review
body:
Love your reviews but You missed two main points :

The ONLY pan in the world that doesn’t form a center bulge when heated . Eg heat an all clad and add oil, the center will bulge and oil will gather at the side. Demeyre stays PERFECTLY FLAT.

It NEVER ever warps. unlike any other such as all clad.

The sauce pans and sauté pans are cladded only till the edges. Demeyere does that on purpose and takes the layers only till the edges and a few mm’s above. This way when u cook soup or tomatoe sauce the edges do not over heat and burn or dry the food that touches the walls.

All my cookware is demeyere, pans pots, saute, etc.

I have never seen a handle issue.
This is clearly a rare defect and evidenced by the fact they replaced it and are sure of their product!
author: @KOZ-k1p

title: The Truth About Demeyere Cookware: My Brutally Honest Review
body: I use cast iron, carbon steel, Le Cruset enameled cast iron and a Demeyer Atlantis stainless steel pan.  Love them all for what they are each good for.  All quality cookware I will be passing on one day.

title: The Truth About Demeyere Cookware: My Brutally Honest Review
body: Great content as always. I think it's worth mentioning (at least if I recall correctly) that Demeyere Atlantis pans are fully sealed. I don't know if you'd want to put them in the dishwasher, but if you do, they won't have the aluminum deterioration issue that All-Clad would have.

title: The Truth About Demeyere Cookware: My Brutally Honest Review
body: I bought 4 demeyere pans last year, largely influenced by this channel, and they are all exceptional. Never had an issue with any of my handles, god forbid, but I just love them. I honestly wish I wouldn’t have bought two Le Creusets before I discovered demeyere. I think one LC is probably enough, and I would wager that demeyere can do anything Le Creuset can do, but better. I do love my LC pots. They’re beautiful and feel special when I use them, but they are overall not as user-friendly.

title: Cornish Cross Dethroned?  Ginger Broiler vs Delaware heritage breed - an honest 9 week review
body: Thanks for the video!  With those fast growing breeds, it’s vital to weigh them every couple weeks and divide them up, or they will get to be all different sizes. The big operations split them into three groups: underweight, overweight, and just right. Don’t necessarily change the amount of feed per bird, just make sure the fatties are competing with each other and not the runts.

title: Cornish Cross Dethroned?  Ginger Broiler vs Delaware heritage breed - an honest 9 week review
body: Last year I got the ginger broilers from mcmurray, and some they called “Cornish roasters”. The roaster is not available this year because of avian flu and I don’t know what strain it was, but it was way better than regular jumbo cornishx, as far as health and legs, and they got really reallly big. The ginger broilers are an awesome free range meat chicken, I still have several of last years hens and a rooster, they make great layers too, and I’m gonna see what happens when I hatch the ginger eggs, I just set some today.

title: Cornish Cross Dethroned?  Ginger Broiler vs Delaware heritage breed - an honest 9 week review
body: the regular Delaware is only good as a slow cooked bird for soup, you can cross a delaware rooster with a new hamphsire hen to make whats called an indian river chicken which is a broiler.
author: @michaelbessey3772

title: Cornish Cross Dethroned?  Ginger Broiler vs Delaware heritage breed - an honest 9 week review
body: The American Buff Orpington is no longer a good dual purpose bird. The breeding stock has gone to crap since it fell out of favor as dual purpose. It also almost hit the endangered species list. It would take a long time and some very selective breeding to get them back to their dual purpose origins. I contemplated looking into getting some European stock since they were maintained overseas, but decided against it. Their meat is not that great IMO. I've stuck with freedom rangers, but I'll be working on an American Bresse cross here soon. I'll be crossing male ABs with Freedom Rangers; I have 3 20 week old hens; 10-12lbs and 4 Breese roos; 2 @ 19 weeks and 2 @ 10. Should be interesting to see what happens with the offspring.
author: @forced4motorsports

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: One word for this video, Tranquility.  The scenes and sounds give a calming effect to the viewers.  Thank you for presenting a half hour of visible peacefulness.
author: @piperleelee

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: Thank you so much for the elegant simplicity in your videos. My father in law comes from a long line of farmers, and now, at 84, he's dying of cancer. He's mostly deaf, so keeping him entertained is a struggle. I had the idea to put farming videos on his tablet that he could just watch and not need to worry about dialogue. His imagination can fill in all the animal sounds. He smiled more broadly at this video than at anything else in the recent past. It's like you've given him his young life back, one video at a time. We thank you. ❤
author: @ACarole

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: Okay this was just BEAUTIFUL. Omg... the heavy fog, the lush green grass, the cows looking nothing short of mjestic, Morgan riding his bike with the LWFD (Large white farm dog)running behind him.. this was just perfect Morgan. Please do more videos like this... this was beautiful
author: @Alicia-fy3je

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: This is what all farms should look like. The madness of factory farming and overproduction is disgusting. This is just beautiful.
author: @bumble8145

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: 24:07 Hey that's me!! It was so amazing to meet you and everyone on the farm Morgan! Peaches the goose is doing wonderful.
author: @mtorrisi42

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: Morgan, the new barn structure is beautiful. I confess I do love the "weird" chickens moment of Zen. Lady Abbyington sure enjoys running alongside you, as you ride your e-bike up to the cattle herd. It seems Amanda Hugginkiss likes her solitude. Its great to see everyone and Allison's kitchen garden, too. Take care!
author: @anitraahrens905

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: I still can not believe that I've found this video, it is truly an enormous luck. It gives you so much so much to think about and calm your mind down at the same time: what a miracle. Slow pace of life of farm is just charming, but it will be inevitable for normal big city-people to let their souls relax a little bit, that's what frustrating. It is a heaven on earth, I nearly cried, frankly speaking I shed dozens of tears... I was touched by the fact that you give them all names, that you treat them so kindly. A very cautious and gentle man - rarity in modern world. Thank you from the bottom of my heart.
author: @qwo1x222

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: I grew up with my grandmother in 🇰🇪, Kenya, Mukurweini Nyeri. These are the sounds I would wake upto for most of my life. Now at 30 years and living in cold and depressed Sweden 🇸🇪 with my family,  I desperately want to have my children experience what peace and tranquility I had while growing up.  Thank you for this video❤
author: @Summerrain55

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: I love seeing how excited the animals get to see you, you clearly have a bond with them all. Your everyday chores and jobs are daily things for you Morgan. For us it's like going on an adventure, and likely one we will never experience in real life.
author: @lms1068

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body:
So glad to see that the tadpoles became adult frogs. Congratulations! Yah! All the cows will get a chance to mingle next week. It was so good to see the wife and garden. Please say hello to her for me. Thanks!
Prayers for health and safety.🙏❤🙏

body: I believe Joey needs to  go with his sisters to elevate this problem, and give Randy the space to be the alpha male cow. It will also make Belinda, and Bonny less anxiety ridden and lonely.

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: My 15 month old LOVED this video so much. She was mesmerized by seeing how the real farm animals look. She was even singing the Old Macdonald song. Thank you for this video!
author: @CamilleO.

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: Wow, the way you take care of the chickens is so professional! [1:30] Seeing them running around happily makes me feel joyful too.
author: @HealingwithAshe

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body:
It was a real treat to enjoy the farm from your perspective, Morgan. No jets flying overhead, no cars or trucks racing down the road. Just the bucolic sounds of nature; grass rustling, baby geese chirping, pigs snorting, macho man mooing, you talking to your animals, them talking back.
The peace de resistance: frogs swimming and croaking in the night, in the pond that you created for them, from the eggs you protected from the birds. Your reward: a nightly symphony of the life you facilitated because nothing says farm and country like the wildlife that emerges from the pond, on a warm summer night in the month of June.
Peace, from Green-Eyed Lady.
author: @scooterpush

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body:
The otherworldlyness of the farm on a foggy summer morning is breathtaking.
It looked like your farm bike was going to suddenly run into Rod Serling as he talked about the rest of your day.
author: @VonPatzy

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: This is a wonderful "moment of zen" video. Just the sound of the farm and your daily routine... You're a very good orator, Morgan, but every now and then a peaceful video of farm life - like this - really adds to the variety of the channel. 👍🦆🐔🐮 PS. Nicely shot and cut too.
author: @malloid

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body:
I put this on for my toddler this morning. She is entranced. I just sat here too long watching this too.

This is great low stim content for her as she can say the animals now. She goes “quack quack” when she sees the ducks.

This was so peaceful
author: @RevivingKayla

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: My almost 3 y-o loves this video! She has limited options for screen time snd this by far is her favorite! We drove past some cows and she said, "Look, momma! Its Macho Man!" Thank you for sharing your life with the world!
author: @kinteab.3268

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: Hello family, here for the fun and stories. Edit: I as typing as I heard at the beginning "Hello Sweetheart" thought you were addressing Allison for a second. Just a reminder not sweethearts are human. I am the exactly the same way and talk like that to my "extended" family..Edit edit: While going to to the ducks and geese either Toby or Abby looked at back at the camera like does this shot make my butt look to big.😂
author: @lydias2012

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body:
Even though there's a lot of work going on here, there's a certain serenity to the entire video that's just beautiful!
Gonna watch this over and over!
author: @mnemosynevermont5524

title: Day in the Life of a Small Farm
body: My toddler loves watching Gold Shaw farm. It’s all he wants to watch now 😂 thank you gold Shaw, we love your channel and your animals ❤
author: @nena313308

title: Day in the Life: D1 College Football Player
body: I feel like it definitely helps to have all these incredible support staff and facilities. If I was a D1 athlete, I would literally feel obligated to win every single game if this was what was behind me.
author: @YourBillionaireBlueprint

title: Day in the Life: D1 College Football Player
body: Not an Oklahoma fan or anything, but I really appreciate you taking the time to make this content.  I found it very entertaining.  Also, I never thought much about the Sooner's facilities, but they're super nice.  I wish you nothing but the best my guy.
author: @URSoDead2Me

title: Day in the Life: D1 College Football Player
body: Thank you for renewing my hope in the younger generation pertaining to faith in our Lord. You are an inspiration to a lot of young adults. Keep up the fantastic work!!
author: @mattdail6294

title: Day in the Life: D1 College Football Player
body: My mom heard what I was watching and said wow that is crazy I think I just needed those words right now! Thanks, man you're awesome. I pray you make NFL!!!
author: @kentjones9280

title: Day in the Life: D1 College Football Player
body: Turk, enjoy seeing your world at OU and glad to see the specialists still mess around like back in the day when I was there with Tony D. and Uwe VonS. (and your facilities are FAR better than we had but we have two National Championship rings 74/75, get after it!)
author: @williamshirk2897

title: Day in the Life: D1 College Football Player
body: Ben Harris making the blog! His mom was the trainer that took care of me when I dislocated my knee way back in 1992!
author: @InformalGreeting

title: Day in the Life: D1 College Football Player
body: Great vlog as always.  And thank you for including the names of some of the new players that are in the vlog as it helps identify who is who.  Lord willing may your arm heal real soon.  Boomer!

title: Day in the Life: D1 College Football Player
body: Ok with why is this random hangtime vid  I saw for the 1 yr of being subbed only when I click most popular

title: Day in the Life: D1 College Football Player
body: Hey Michael, I’m just here to thank you for the content you put out. Recently I’ve been waking up at 4:30 and watching your Bible studies before school starts.
author: @danielchiddister9221

title: Day in the Life: D1 College Football Player
body: Dunno why this got suggested but enjoyed it. So different to university sport in the Uk. Feel for ya with the broken hand I did my hand/thumb/arm 6 years in a row after I left university and ended up having to retire early. Gets tough mentally it’s a dark place been injured. Keep going bro
author: @Nik2555

title: Day in the Life: D1 College Football Player
body: man i remember this like it was ysterday when hangtime was still a cutie patootie until now though im glad your channel is still big mike!
author: @jaczey

title: Day in the Life: D1 College Football Player
body: I hope to one day catch a college football game live. The atmosphere will be sooo good. Also everytime I watch a Sooner game on TV, Boomer Sooner gets stuck in my head for the next week xD. Unfortunately in Germany we only get two to four (I believe) games per week. Hope to see you in the NFL soon ^^
author: @Biglmpact

title: Day in the Life: D1 College Football Player
body: Turk I love that you stayed on another year when you didn't have to, it shows your love and commitment to the team and fans.
author: @dementiabiden1621

body: Warehouses that let you listen to music and dont trip about you going to the rest room be the best
author: @markow.3054

title: a day in the life of an AMAZON WAREHOUSE PACKER in 60 secs #shorts
body: You missed the part when you woke up in the dark to go to work. Then come back home in the dark. It feels like the entire day is wasyed
author: @damilolaowolabi6716

title: a day in the life of an AMAZON WAREHOUSE PACKER in 60 secs #shorts
body: Simple repetition jobs like this seem chill and good at the start but get old really quick. I need to use my brain and solve problems
author: @codycurnutte9778

title: a day in the life of an AMAZON WAREHOUSE PACKER in 60 secs #shorts
body: Looks good for me, I like not being bothered when working. Just get in, do my shit and get out
author: @GriffRP99

title: a day in the life of an AMAZON WAREHOUSE PACKER in 60 secs #shorts
body:
The Amazon warehouse I used to work at was so crowded, by the time you got outside for your break, it was already over...
Truly was one of the most soul crushing jobs I'd ever had
author: @jayceegee258

title: a day in the life of an AMAZON WAREHOUSE PACKER in 60 secs #shorts
body: I just started at Amazon as a flex in grocery and it has it’s good and bad just like any other job. Love the weekly pay.
author: @MsStarbright84

title: a day in the life of an AMAZON WAREHOUSE PACKER in 60 secs #shorts
body:
As an Introvert. I like this a lot. It's like the previous warehouse Job I had working in the cold storage. In Australia the pay is different and much more higher for those working in a warehouse compared to other countries.
After a while I had some health issues going on that weren't related to work.
I had to deal with it. But now all is good.
Warehouse Jobs do look lifeless and boring but I do well at those type of jobs 😊
author: @7NumbersLeft

title: a day in the life of an AMAZON WAREHOUSE PACKER in 60 secs #shorts
body: Thanks for your work. I'm sure the job can be bleak and boring, but you've certainly helped my family and I dozens, if not hundreds of times over the years.
author: @RoninRAB

title: a day in the life of an AMAZON WAREHOUSE PACKER in 60 secs #shorts
body: Felt like it was me at work especially when you said you’re on your second break 😅 the day goes by so slow after that last break 😩
author: @a1sobossy

title: a day in the life of an AMAZON WAREHOUSE PACKER in 60 secs #shorts
body: When I think of Alienation, the sense and feeling of dissattachment from the work you produce by karl marx, this is what comes to mind.
author: @ameridesign

title: a day in the life of an AMAZON WAREHOUSE PACKER in 60 secs #shorts
body: Yeaaaah I left my first day lol. Picking was not fit me, for 12 hours? Couldn’t do it. Now I’m back working from the comfort of my home, I forgot all about the nuisances of working in person. Packing a lunch, waking up early to dress, rushing to get gas… now I’m back to waking up 30 min before shift to make breakfast and clock in.
author: @CeceDeAnn

title: Day in the Life | Electrician Apprentice #shorts #constructionwork #apprentice #buildingmindgrasp
body: Electrical apprentice for 5 years, recently retired from it. It is GREAT to see a woman in the trade. Good luck with the career
author: @paulbell1522

title: Day in the Life | Electrician Apprentice #shorts #constructionwork #apprentice #buildingmindgrasp
body: 45 year retired electrician here, This is one of the best career paths you could have taken in these times. After you become proficient in installations, try to steer toward electrical maintenance and trouble shooting. It's easier on the bones and very satisfying. You'll be a hero to somebody every day.
author: @hotratz69

title: Day in the Life | Electrician Apprentice #shorts #constructionwork #apprentice #buildingmindgrasp
body: Just started as an apprentice at 30 and I relate so much with everything in this video. So nice to see a woman killing it in the trades too. Proud of you!
author: @stillhaze

title: Day in the Life | Electrician Apprentice #shorts #constructionwork #apprentice #buildingmindgrasp
body: Learning a trade is one of smartest thing anyone could do.  I been doing carpentry work for almost 30 years and I've been able to provide a life for my daughter that I only dreamed of when I was her age.  Great work! Wish you all the best in life
author: @derek234

title: Day in the Life | Electrician Apprentice #shorts #constructionwork #apprentice #buildingmindgrasp
body: I have run the wrong size wire before. I remembered it while I was at home. I drove back to work at 8pm, pulled that wire and put the right size in. Done at 11pm. Went home and was back up at 3am for another day. Good job girl!
author: @CrankyOldMachinist

title: Day in the Life | Electrician Apprentice #shorts #constructionwork #apprentice #buildingmindgrasp
body: Good, I’m an electrician for 21 years and my wife is my helper now for the last six years can’t ask for a better helper. Keep on going
author: @yadielbalboa3035

title: Day in the Life | Electrician Apprentice #shorts #constructionwork #apprentice #buildingmindgrasp
body: I'm really nervous to try to become an apprentice somewhere locally, but this gives me hope! I always thought I would go to college, get a degree, have an office or even a hospital job and make good money that way- but the trades are in such high demand right now! It's awesome to see a woman in the trades making progress and showing others how it's done :)
author: @nahfam018

title: Day in the Life | Electrician Apprentice #shorts #constructionwork #apprentice #buildingmindgrasp
body: Electrical is so hard tho 😭I work at Home Depot and that dept seems so complicated and intimidating but with proper schooling I'm sure it's an amazing job! This is inspiring !
author: @luna_nav377

title: Day in the Life | Electrician Apprentice #shorts #constructionwork #apprentice #buildingmindgrasp
body: That "realize you messed something up while on lunch and now you can't stop thinking about how you'll fix it so you head back early" moment is very relatable.
author: @DustinKBoggs

title: Day in the Life | Electrician Apprentice #shorts #constructionwork #apprentice #buildingmindgrasp
body: I've been in the trade 41 years, I am so glad to see young people getting in this field, don't be afraid to sweat, get bruised, form calluses and put up with other contractor BS (plumbers, framers and drywallers) - did my apprenticeship in 1984 - I have no regrets, the future is yours to make
author: @ricb1261

title: Day in the Life | Electrician Apprentice #shorts #constructionwork #apprentice #buildingmindgrasp
body: Keep it up! This is great to see. Keep posting as you go. I'm looking at going into the trades but I'm currently in distribution management. Great stuff.
author: @AM-fn9gk

body: Got my license at 25, started when I was 19-20, they put us in the ringer while we apprentices 💀

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: The fact you were able to even film this shows me how relaxed this academy is lol. My academy wouldnt even let us have our phones on our person for the entire 21 weeks we were there. No hate or anything though, we all do the same job in the end so keep striving man
author: @dylanbradley7583

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: I graduated from the academy in 2011. Although I didn't get hired on with law enforcement, it was one of the best achievements in my life. Very proud of it.
author: @LiLgPnoy15

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: This academy is so relaxed man compared to mine. We got screamed and yelled at for the first two months then after a while we earned little privileges like snack time and stuff. I didn’t get to go out and get lunch at all we had to bring lunch we had no refrigerator or a microwave. Plus I had to shave my head and couldn’t start growing my hair back until the middle of the Academy but my hair couldn’t be but a inch and a half long military fade. I missed my waves and I’m still trying to get my waves back lol.
author: @jackwashington8664

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: Going to the academy on January 17th , Thanks to the lord! This video helped a lot bro. Keep doing what you do.
author: @Jeremyx21

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body:
Honestly you have motivated me so
Much and I don’t regret my decision in becoming a police officer in the future.
author: @officialdioptech

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: I appreciate you so much for this! I am in the background investigation phase for an agency in Florida and would be starting academy in April and this was a really good way to get an idea of sort of things to expect
author: @NikoLeftVods

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: Good stuff man. I appreciate the energy at your academy. Your boy in your class that is sitting next to you definitely trains pulling his sidearm when he is watching TV. That boy is ready for patrol. Best luck to all of you.
author: @theoppenheimerprojects2867

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: What kind of relaxed police academy was this?!?  Bdu's, polo shirts?? Wow.  Lots have changed since I went to the academy in 92😊
author: @rodzilla355

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: I am currently going through my post secondary education needed before I head to the academy, this video made me feel excited and optimistic and It’s cool to see so many others who feel the same
author: @Boxingbc03

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: It’s insane how different your academy is from what mine was lol. We had to had our heads shaved, no facial hair, in formation at 0615 every morning. God forbid someone had a phone or a chick fil a cup the instructors saw we would’ve been pt-ing til sundown
author: @GoldGlov3

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: Wow, never seen a non-stress academy before. In our academy, we'd assemble and march into the classrooms, subject to any scrutiny unless actually in classroom or C7, we were pretty much at some form of attention, all 26 weeks. And there was never such thing as "I don't plan on running."🤣
author: @kettlekorn471

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: This is so cool!! I currently live in Louisiana and I’m trying to get into law enforcement too so I’m trying to get on with another department but I’ve always wondered and thought about doing a vlog but I just don’t know what to really talk about and I’m kinda camera shy so I’m very excited to see this!!
author: @nicholassanders8163

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: Great video.  I was a Police Officer in Baltimore City, Maryland for 20 years.  I was a Marine for 20 years I’m retired and wish I had never left

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: Man I’m a cadet now and I can’t wait till I go to the academy and get it over with

title: BRUTALLY Honest Review of THE UNIVERSITY OF YORK - Is York University ACTUALLY Good or Not?
body: Nice to know the new development of the University. Lots of changes and huge additions to thr new campus in the East. I was a graduate students back in the late 80's and early 90's. Planning to come back for a visit with my kids.
author: @peterwhungwong74

title: BRUTALLY Honest Review of THE UNIVERSITY OF YORK - Is York University ACTUALLY Good or Not?
body: I graduated from York (Goodricke College) in 1995. The comment about 'community feel' reflected my experience. It's an expensive city but it is very safe.
author: @plandseer4527

title: BRUTALLY Honest Review of THE UNIVERSITY OF YORK - Is York University ACTUALLY Good or Not?
body: I did my master in York from 2014 to 2015 and it was the best time of my life. I've studied in two more universities and York is definitely my favourite. I also loved the town and the atmosphere. I lived on campus in Constantine college, the best college! :)
author: @royarav10

title: BRUTALLY Honest Review of THE UNIVERSITY OF YORK - Is York University ACTUALLY Good or Not?
body: Love your content, I finished my degree years ago but I just love studying. Given the choice I would have spent my whole adult life jumping from one degree to the next. Keep it up.
author: @DrDrColin295

title: BRUTALLY Honest Review of THE UNIVERSITY OF YORK - Is York University ACTUALLY Good or Not?
body: thank you for the review! im from indonesia & currently preparing myself to get an international fully-funded student exchange programme by our govt. im considering uni of york as my first choice. i hope i get accepted and be there soon! ;)

title: BRUTALLY Honest Review of THE UNIVERSITY OF YORK - Is York University ACTUALLY Good or Not?
body: Hey man, thanks for the memories. Attended DERS at York during 2000-03 (Langwith College) and glad to see that a lot has changed on campus. BTW back in my days James was postgrad. college only. Also, back then the university was constantly ranked among the top10 in the major league tables- obviously towards the bottom 10 -so a bit disappointed to see it has slipped quite a lot over the last 20 years.

title: Ultimate Boston Campus Tour #boston #mit #bostoncollege #bu
body: Why should we take you opinion on any of your reviews. What are your qualifications? Who asked you to do this? What’s your major? How long have you been attending the academic institution you’ve been enrolled in? Are you a professional student? So just because you know these institutions of higher learning doesn’t mean you know the bings that are important to me specifically. Thanks, but no thanks!
